subject: Geology
title: Shield Volcanoes
description: A quick, practical guide to Shield Volcanoes.

hook.text: Mauna Loa in Hawaii is the largest active volcano on Earth, but it's so broad and gentle you barely notice the slope.

buildup.text: Shield volcanoes are wide, gently sloping cones built from many layers of fluid basaltic lava flows.

discovery.text: Their shape resembles a warrior's shield lying flat. The low viscosity of basalt lets lava spread far before cooling.

twist.text: Despite looking calm, shield volcanoes can produce enormous eruptions. Mauna Loa has buried roads and towns in lava.

climax.text: Shield volcanoes build the biggest structures on Earth. Mauna Loa, measured from its ocean base, is taller than Everest.

punchline.text: The gentlest volcanoes are the biggest.

quiz.question: What type of lava builds shield volcanoes?
